meta.house: WEF Sporthorse Auction powered by VDL Stud

horses.name: VDL Mette
horses.catalog_text:
As a three-year-old, VDL Mette already demonstrated her exceptional qualities by claiming the championship title at the Dutch Mare Championship. The remarkable abilities she showcased during free jumping at that time proved to be a perfect indicator of her potential as a sport horse. VDL Mette is an athletic mare that jumps with a very quick take off, is very careful, has a great technique and has athletic scope. VDL Mette is a winner in the ring and is an eyecatcher too!

Sire: As a young horse, sire Etoulon VDL already impressed with his remarkable jumping technique and reflexes. He continued his outstanding performances in international classes with multiple placings on 1.50m level with Jur Vrieling. Equally impressive are Etoulon VDL’s breeding achievements, with his oldest offspring excelling at the highest levels, such as Jordan Molga M, Ebanking, Opaline de W&S, and Lexington.

Dam: Dam Fresia van Olivia VDL, combines the influential sire Cardento with a strong French damline. As a three-year-old, she earned high marks at the mare inspection and again in the mare performance test.
Damline: VDL Mette traces back in the third generation to the full sister of the renowned French sire Papillon Rouge, who competed at the highest level under Xavier Leredde. Great-granddam Olivia Rouge has produced the 1.50m-level stallion Dragon Rouge, and from this direct branch also originates top-level showjumpers such as Quartz Rouge, Cincaba Rouge, and Nathan des Hayettes.

horses.name: VDL Night Grey
horses.catalog_text:
VDL Night Grey is a direct son of the great European Champion mare Cella. VDL Night Grey is a tall gelding with a top mentality and the perfect amount of blood. Very straight forward and brave in the ring, he is a real pleaser and gives his rider confidence. He jumps with a very good technique, is careful and has scope. VDL Night Grey is a great Amateur horse but is also a great horse to produce for the bigger sport.

Sire: BWP ambassador Falaise de Muze comes from an exceptional damline and is a direct son of Harrie Smolders’ former top showjumping mare Walnut de Muze. Falaise de Muze himself performed admirably at 1.50m-level and has proven his value in breeding, sire to successful 1.60m-level showjumpers such as Ivory TCS, Just Special VK, and Hurricane DD.

Dam: Under Ben Maher, dam Cella secured major victories during her career, including the 5*-Grand Prix of London, the 3*-Grand Prixs of Wellington and Tampa, the World Cup competition of CSI5* Wellington, and the Nations Cup at CSIO5* Dublin. This extraordinary mare claimed a total of 13 international wins and earned team gold and individual silver at the European Championships in Herning. In recognition of her stellar career, she was crowned FEI Horse of the Year in 2013. Before her remarkable sporting achievements, Cella produced four international showjumpers, including the 1.50/1.60m-level performers Happy Wandor van ’t Roosakker, Fabio van ’t Roosakker and Handor van ’t Roosakker.

Damline: VDL Night Grey hails from the prestigious Van ’t Roosakker lineage, known for producing a multitude of international showjumpers. These include top-level performers such as Zarkava Hero Z, Electra van ’t Roosakker, Kaprice, H5 Ganesh Hero Z, VDL Groep Elegant Hero Z, Attoucha, Babbe van ’t Roosakker, Emperio van ’t Roosakker, Equador van ’t Roosakker, Giorgio van ’t Hagenhof, Erco van ’t Roosakker, and Athena.

horses.name: VDL Obsession
horses.catalog_text:
VDL Obsession is a son of rising top Sire Carrera who competed at highest level with Jur Vrieling. VDL Obsession is an extra addition to the WEF Auction. This super talented 6 year old has everything to become a horse for the modern top sport. He has a great body, length, good height and an elegant look. He has a light athletic canter, has blood but is easy to ride. When he starts jumping he showcases a great technique, extra use of his body, quick reflexes and lots of athletic scope. VDL Obsession has all the ability to be a horse for the top sport!

Sire: With a career at the highest level and multiple Grand Prix victories, sire Carrera VDL has lived up to the expectations. This genetically valuable stallion has also proven to be an asset to breeding, with his offspring including successful top-level competitors such as Greatest Boy, Jup, Komboy, Good Farming HS, and Imar. 

Dam: Dam Lumen Ulady competed internationally for years with Remco Been and Hans van der Kolk. She reached the 3* 1.55m-level and achieved notable results, including a victory in a 1.40m class at CSI3* Herning, a second place in the 1.45m class at CSI4* Jumping Amsterdam, and a third-place finish in the Grand Prix of CSI2* Zuidwolde. Lumen Ulady is, just like the Olympic medalists Authentic and Ninja la Silla, sired by the renowned sire Guidam.

Damline: Obsession comes from a versatile Dutch damline and has the same granddam as the international 1.60m-level performer Fellini 131, ridden by Jonathan Gordon. Other top-level horses from this direct damline include Valentijn of Andrius Petrovas, Whin Whin of Dominic Webb, and Vince of Jimmy Torano.

horses.name: VDL Nheintje
horses.catalog_text:
VDL Nheintje is a daughter of Comthago VDL, who is not only competing at the highest level and won Silver Medal at the Dutch Championship but proving to pass those qualities on as a sire. VDL Nheintje will be a great partner to go through the different levels. She has a great mind, is competitive and has scope. She jumps with a good technique and has a quick take off. Along with her good scope, she is proving that she is a real “tryer” under saddle.  

Sire: Under the saddle of Hessel Hoekstra, sire Comthago VDL achieved already remarkable results. Last year, at just ten years old, he won the 4*-Grand Prix of Ommen, placed second in the World Cup competition at CSI4* Poznan, and came fourth in the Grand Prix of CSI3* Leeuwarden. The Holsteiner son of Comme Il Faut has also made a promising start to his breeding career, and VDL Nheintje comes from his first crop.

Dam: Peliana, the dam of VDL Nheintje, carries the valuable performance genes of World Cup winner Libero H (Jos Lansink), who has also proven to be an exceptional sire. Peliana herself has produced several successful offspring, including the international 1.45m-level showjumper Zidane 19, ridden by Karl Brocks.

Damline: VDL Nheintje descends from a traditional Dutch damline, which provides a solid foundation. The granddam, Deliana, produced the 1.45m-level showjumper Free Willy, ridden by Leonardo Tipo Galbani, and is also found in the second generation of Sally Goding’s 1.60m-level performer Spring Willow.

horses.name: VDL O What A Girl
horses.catalog_text:
Talented and desired from the day she was born, that is VDL O What A Girl. She has consistently showcased her exceptional talent in both national and international young horse competitions. She claimed victory in the final of the prestigious Dutch GMB Competition. At the National Championships, she finished 10th in the Final and qualified for the World Championships in Lanaken by jumping only clear rounds.  O What A Girl is a mare with a great sport type, good length in her body and a incredible balanced canter. At the jumps she is very clever, careful, with a quick take off and a very good technique. All of this, plus a lot of scope, makes her a top talent not only for the big sport but will also make her a great horse for Junior and Young rider levels. VDL O What A Girl! The name says it all!  Grand Prix potential!

Sire: Sire Bacardi VDL enjoyed a distinguished sport career under Jos Lansink and Janika Sprunger. As a seven-year-old, he claimed silver at the World Championships in Lanaken, before advancing to the highest level of the sport with Janika Sprunger. Bacardi VDL delivered outstanding performances in Grand Prixs, Nations Cups, and World Cup competitions, and took part in the Tryon World Equestrian Games. Besides his impressive sport career, Bacardi VDL is establishing himself as an exceptional sire, producing top-level showjumpers such as Gigi-Carmen and Goldenstar.

Dam: As a three-year-old, dam It’s A Girl GR made history being crowned national champion at the Dutch Mare Championship. She went on to prove her exceptional qualities under saddle, excelling in the mare performance test with scores of 9.5 for reflexes and technique, along with three additional scores of 9. One of her first offspring is the international showjumper News Flash VDL, who delivered impressive results last year in the six-year-old classes under Pello Elorduy Ibarzabal.

Damline: The granddam, Purple G, is a half-sister to the international 1.55m-level performer Find Touch

horses.name: VDL Omthago
horses.catalog_text:
VDL Omthago is the name of this son of the great Comthago VDL, who is very succesful at the highest International level with Hessel Hoekstra (the pair was Silver medal winner at the Dutch National Championship). As a 5-year-old, VDL Omthago jumped an incredible amount of clear rounds and Finished 7th in the National Championship for 5 year olds in Holland. He qualified for the World Championships, where he once again only jumped clear rounds and finished 8th place in the Final. VDL Omthago is a horse that grows in the ring, a horse that wants to perform. He has a very modern type, good length in the body and a very adjustable canter. He jumps with a very good technique, very quick take off, is very careful and has a lot of athletic scope. Never spectacular, but whether you build them small or big, the jump is always the same!  VDL Omthago has all the ingredients to become your Grand Prix horse for the future.

Sire: Under Hessel Hoekstra, sire Comthago VDL achieved already remarkable results. Last year, at just ten years old, he won Silver medal at the National Championship, he won the 4*-Grand Prix of Ommen, placed second in the World Cup competition at CSI4* Poznan, and came fourth in the Grand Prix of CSI3* Leeuwarden. The Holsteiner son of Comme Il Faut has also made a promising start to his breeding career, and VDL Omthago comes from his second crop.

Dam: Justine VDE, the dam of VDL Omthago, combines the valuable sport and breeding stallions Harley VDL and Libero H. She is a half-sister to the 1.40/1.45m-level showjumpers Endy, ridden by Adam Prudent, and Igor ridden by Carly Anthony.

Damline: This allround Dutch damline has produced a number of international sport horses. Closely related to VDL Omthago is, among others, the 1.45m-Grand Prix horse Ingar Endy VDE of rider Xin Xu.

horses.name: VDL Omica
horses.catalog_text:
VDL Omica is a raw diamond. He may be a bit green for his age, but he shows a lot of potential. He is a very modern built sporthorse with a good length in his body. VDL Omica is a horse with a comfortable canter, he is careful at the jump and has a lot of scope. VDL Omica has the potential to be your talented young horse for the future.

Sire: Under the saddle of Hessel Hoekstra, sire Comthago VDL has already achieved remarkable results. Last year, at just ten years old, he won the 4*-Grand Prix of Ommen, placed second in the World Cup competition at CSI4* Poznan, and came fourth in the Grand Prix of CSI3* Leeuwarden. The Holsteiner son of Comme Il Faut has also made a promising start to his breeding career, and VDL Omica comes from his second crop.

Dam: Dam Tinca has already produced multiple international sport horses, including the top-level showjumper Dilano, with whom Ahmed Naser Elnaggar won the World Cup competition at CSI2* Cairo, and the 1.50m-level showjumper Amanda, ridden by Aleksandra Pushkarskaya.

Damline: This is the lineage of the legendary showjumper Authentic, who secured two team gold medals and individual bronze competing at the Athens and Beijing Olympics with Beezie Madden. This direct damline also produced the successful Grand Prix horses About A Dream, ridden by Pius Schwizer, and Olinda of Todd Minikus. Other 1.50/1.60m-level competing horses from this line include Julius.D, Ibiza D, Kardento R, and Baby Love.

horses.name: VDL Quinta
horses.catalog_text:
VDL Quinta is a promising daughter of Quickthago VDL. She is very light to ride with a very athletic canter. She jumps with a great technique and has lot of reach on the jump. VDL Quinta is a mare with scope and the right mentality. VDL Quinta is a mare that has the potential to become not only a great amateur horse but one for a professional to be a winner at National Grand Prix level.

Sire: The Oldenburg-licensed premium stallion Quickthago VDL boasts an excellent pedigree and is currently performing successfully under junior rider Milan Morssinkhof, with whom he participated in the European Championship last year. VDL Quinta-KL comes from the first crop of this son of multiple Grand Prix winner Quickly de Kreisker.
Dam: His dam, Emma VI, is a half-sister to the international 1.50m-level showjumper Casalca Z ridden by Jens Wickström. Through her sire, Cassilino, she carries the valuable genes of top progenitor Cassini I.
Damline: Stemming from Holsteiner damline number 1298 where the granddam Ziance I is a half-sister to the Holsteiner Verband stallion Chezarro. This is the same damline as, for example, the Grand Prix stallions Emilion and BG Conquisto, as well as other top-level horses such as Zaccorado Blue, Caletto Cabana, Non Stop SV, and Antonio Vozone’s European Championship horse Lacy Woman.

horses.name: VDL Morning Star
horses.catalog_text:
VDL Morning Star is a son of Carrera crossed with the mare Watch Me B, who is the mother to Ibiza - the Grand Prix horse ridden by Jenny Rankin. Ibiza was sold in a previous edition of the WEF Sport Horse Auction. VDL Morning Star is very good to ride with a well balanced cater. He jumps with good reflex, good technique and has all the scope you could desire. VDL Morning Star has a bright future and could potentially be your next Grand Prix horse.

Sire: Carrera VDL has lived up to the high expectations placed on him with multiple Grand Prix victories under Jur Vrieling and is now making a significant impact in breeding. He has already produced successful offspring such as Greatest Boy, Jup, Komboy, Good Farming HS, and Imar.

Dam: Dam Watch Me B, has already produced two internationally successful offspring: Ibiza, who competes with Jenny Ranking in the 1.55m-classes and was runner-up in the Nations Cup final in Warsaw last autumn, and Diamond JWB, who is performing at 1.40m-level. Watch Me B herself is a half-sister to the Grand Prix stallion Zapatero VDL, ridden by Jessica Kürten.

Damline: Granddam Manieta is a half-sister to the 1.45m-level showjumpers Orlando, Nicky, and Trouble Maker. Other closely related family members such as Joviaal ES, Lorenzo ES, Zarvil, Djoel, Fendi, Jay Jay ES, Ilyon ES, Janieta P, Curprice, and Discovery have all performed at a minimum of 1.45m-level.

horses.name: VDL Icarronne
horses.catalog_text:
VDL Icarronne has been successfully competing on international 1.50m-level with Eric and Maikel van der Vleuten, showcasing an impressive track record and earning her place as one of the highlights of this collection. Icarronne is horse that can be competitive in all Junior, Young rider, U25, High Amateur, National Grand Prix and International Grand Prix Level!

Sire: Carrera VDL has fulfilled high expectations with multiple Grand Prix victories under Jur Vrieling and is now leaving his mark as a top sire. His offspring include successful top-level competitors such as Greatest Boy, Jup, Komboy, Good Farming HS, and Imar.

Dam: Dam Elordonne-S competed up to 1.40m-level and is a full sister to the international 1.60m-level  horse Loyd, with whom Alessandra Reich participated in the European Championships in Riesenbeck in 2021 and secured a fourth-place finish in the Grand Prix of CSI3* Herzlake. Elordonne-S is also a sibling to the international 1.45m-level horse Dyonne-S, ridden by Giulia Martinengo Marquet.

Damline: VDL Icarronne is the first of four consecutive mares in this damline who competed at a minimum of 1.30m-level in the sport, highlighting the exceptional quality and competitive spirit of the horses from this lineage. In the third generation, this line traces back to a half-sister of the renowned Grand Prix showjumper Virtual Village It’s Otto, who achieved great success with Geoff Billington in the 1990s, including participation in the Atlanta Olympics and the WEG in Rome.

horses.name: VDL Plesman
horses.catalog_text:
This very promissing youngster is just turning 5 this year but certainly has a bright future in front of him! Father Glasgow van’t Merelsnest has proven himself as a Nations Cup horse with his rider Jur Vrieling representing The Dutch Team on numerous occassions. VDL Plesman is from the same mother as World Cup Quailfier winner Farell ridden by Irish Top rider Daniel Coyle and VDL Gouvernante who was succesfull up to 1.50m level with Marlon Zanotelli. VDL Plesman is a horse that is easy to ride, has a comfortable canter and a very good overview at the jump. He jumps with a very good technique, very good use of his body and has a lot of athletic scope.  VDL Plesman is a horse with great potential!

Sire: Glasgow van 't Merelsnest has proven the excellence of his pedigree with outstanding performances at the highest level under Jur Vrieling. The Grand Prix, Nations Cup, and World Cup winner represented the Netherlands at the European Championship in Gothenburg and the World Equestrian Games in Tryon. His oldest offspring have made a mark at the highest level, such as the successful High Five, ridden by Fernando Martinez Sommer, and Imperial HBF ridden by Tim Gredley.

Dam: The valuable Stakkato daughter Becherry has consistently proven to produce top-level showjumpers. VDL Plesman is a half-brother to several outstanding horses, including the 1.60m-level competitor Farrel VDL, with whom Daniel Coyle won World Cup classes in Ocala and Vancouver, and the 1.50m-level performer Gouvernante VDL, ridden by Marlon Modolo Zanotelli.

Damline: VDL Plesman comes from a strong Hanoverian family, which has also produced the top-level showjumper Polyfino ridden by Raimo Aaltonen. The 1.45m-level competing Lacros VDL of Francois Lamontagne, is closely related to VDL Plesman.

horses.name: VDL Norberto
horses.catalog_text:
VDL Noberto is a 6 year old gelding by Norway de la Lande. This very modern looking young horse is built to be successful. His great uphill athletic canter has very good reach and he is good to ride. He jumps with a very good technique, very good use of the body and is very quick from the ground. At the jump, he shows a lot of scope and reach. VDL Noberto is a potential Grand Prix horse.

Sire: The French stallion Norway de la Lande has showcased his abilities up to international 1.50m-level under Franck Schillewaert and has proven to be an excellent sire. His offspring competing at the highest level include Arome, A La Miss de Busseol, Valentine Belmaniere, and Elioth de la Lande. Norway de la Lande was bred from the same dam as the successful Grand Prix stallion Valmy de la Lande (Jack Whitaker).

Dam: Dam Valerie is sired by the renowned breeding stallion Valentino, whose progeny, including Vittorio 8, Vitiki, Vancouver Dreams, and Valut 2, have excelled at the highest levels. Valerie is herself a half-sister to the international 1.55m-level performing Carracio 2 (ridden by Lara-Marie Juraske).

Damline: From this Hanoverian damline originate various accomplished showjumpers, including the 1.40m- and 1.45m-level jumping horses Electric Socks, Electric Ernst and Pan Am.

horses.name: VDL Lancasten
horses.catalog_text:
VDL Lancasten is a son of the upcoming top producer Etoulon VDL crossed with the mare Gouvernante VDL who was successful at 1.50m Level with Marlon Zanotelli. VDL Lancesten has already showed at International Level and is a horse that is great to ride. He is tall but has a very easy comfortable canter. He has a good technique, good use of his body, and has plenty scope. Lancesten VDL is certainly a horse to jump bigger classes on.

Sire: As a young horse, sire Etoulon VDL already impressed with his remarkable jumping technique and reflexes. He continued his outstanding performances in international classes with multiple placings on 1.50m level with Jur Vrieling. Equally impressive are Etoulon VDL’s breeding achievements, with his oldest offspring excelling at the highest levels, such as Jordan Molga M, Ebanking, Opaline de W&S, and Lexington.

Dam: Gouvernante VDL has firmly established herself in the sport with impressive performances at international 1.50m-level under Marlon Modolo Zanotelli. She also enjoyed success with Darragh Kenny, winning two CSI5* classes in Wellington in 2022. Gouvernante VDL is a half-sister to the multiple World Cup winner Farrel VDL, with whom Daniel Coyle triumphed in Ocala and Vancouver.

Damline: This Hanoverian family has produced several successful showjumpers, including the top-level showjumper Polyfino, ridden by Raimo Aaltonen. The 1.50m-level jumping horse Payton, ridden by Willem Greve, also originates from this damline. Lancasten VDL seems to have all the potential to become the next top performer from this impressive family.

horses.name: VDL Nominaal
horses.catalog_text:
VDL Nominaal has already made his debut at the International Young horse level. VDL Nominaal is a son of Twister de la Pomme who is a combination of two true legends of showjumping - Darco (Ludo Phillpaerts) and For Pleasure (Marcus Ehning). Nominaal VDL is such a solid, easy and comfortable jumper. With his looks and beautiful model, he is your ideal partner for the future.

Sire: The genetically valuable sire Twister de la Pomme competed at the highest level with Wesley Mulder and Meike Zwartjens. He is a grandson of the legendary mare Fragance de Chalus. Twister de la Pomme is already making his mark in breeding. Despite having a relatively small number of offspring, his oldest progeny such as T Gavoli Z, Tanja, and Jimmy, are already following in his footsteps at 1.50m-level.

Dam: Dam Hezwiggy JG is a half-sister to several 1.30/1.35m-level showjumpers, including the internationally performing Mijzelf JG, who competed in his first 1.35m classes last year as a seven-year-old.

Damline: VDL Nominaal hails from the esteemed Twiggy line, which has produced numerous outstanding performers. This direct lineage includes 1.45/1.50m-level jumpers such as Carambo Z and Corbiere, and from this family originate top-level competitors like Rochefort, Herbert, Undiamo, Sil, and Vineyard.
